transaction_velocity.notes: Active institutional buyer presence and land assemblage deals, but recent 2025 closed comp data is thin. Mag Mile Capital closed a record $223.5M bridge loan (March 2026) for 636-unit mixed-use development, signaling capital is moving. Berkadia arranged $84M construction bridge for Astor Park. Walker & Dunlop previously arranged $227M sale. Most land comp pricing from 2021-2023 ($319/sf land basis). Development site activity (Native Realty, Continua Developments) suggests sustained institutional conviction.

comps.notes: Native Realty land assemblage for Class A multifamily
Midtown PAL Flagler Village LLC for Astor Park development

distress_level.notes: No material distress signals found in Flagler Village specifically. Florida/Broward lis pendens activity is low for institutional multifamily; residential filings number 'several dozen per week' county-wide but no commercial multifamily distress stories surfaced. Market is healthy with active capital deployment — distressed acquisition opportunities are minimal in this submarket currently.

major_projects.name: FAT Village (Hines + Urban Street Development)
major_projects.notes: $500M mixed-use master plan on 5.6 acres; 24-story and 13-story towers topped out; first units mid-2026, full delivery 2027. One building targets affordable at $1,900/mo entry; other is luxury.

major_projects.name: Astor Park Flagler Village
major_projects.notes: Luxury mid-rise, topped out Oct 2025; $84M Berkadia bridge. Delivering mid-2026.

major_projects.name: Society Las Olas Phase II (Property Markets Group)
major_projects.notes: 42-story tower; nearby but not strictly Flagler Village. Adds to metro supply pressure.

rent_growth.notes: Average Flagler Village rent $2,824 (Dec 2025), down 4% YoY from $2,943. Studios counterintuitively up 11% YoY; 1BR down 7%, 2BR down 2%. Move-in specials visible on apartment listing sites. Supply wave from 2025-2026 deliveries is putting pressure on larger units. Landlords offering light concessions to lease-up new product.

major_moves.notes: +6% YoY employment growth in professional/business services as of Q2 2025

demand_drivers.notes: Fort Lauderdale is a top migration destination — NY, DC, SF, LA/SD all sending residents. Broward added 3,400 nonfarm jobs YoY through Dec 2025. Downtown FTL projected 26-28% population growth by 2030. Professional services growing 6%+ YoY. Strong structural renter demand supports long-term underwriting, even through near-term supply absorption.

capital_availability.notes: March 2026: Mag Mile Capital closed record $223.5M bridge loan for 636-unit FTL mixed-use — signals strong bridge capital appetite. Berkadia arranged $84M construction bridge for Astor Park. FTL cap rate at 6.27% and commercial mortgage rate ~5.11% (Oct 2025) provides positive leverage potential. Multifamily transaction activity rebounded in 2025 nationally, agency lenders active. Banks selective on new construction but refinance and stabilized deals getting done.

regulatory_favorability.notes: Florida state law has preempted local rent control since 1977 — zero rent control risk. Flagler Village sits in an active Opportunity Zone with documented investment activity (15-parcel OZ assemblage on N. Andrews Ave; multiple OZ projects active). Fort Lauderdale city government is pro-development. No material impact fee increases found. Property tax environment stable. OZ investors active through at least 2026 given QOF extension provisions.

key_findings.finding: Massive near-term supply wave: 1,525+ multifamily units delivering in Flagler Village/adjacent Downtown in 2026-2027, led by the $500M FAT Village project (600 units) and Astor Park (252 units). This is the dominant risk to near-term acquisition underwriting.

Rents softening: Average Flagler Village rent fell 4% YoY to $2,824 (Dec 2025) from $2,943. 1BR rents down 7% YoY. Light-to-moderate concessions visible. Supply pressure from 2025-2026 deliveries is real and measurable.

Record $223.5M bridge loan closed March 2026 for 636-unit Fort Lauderdale multifamily/retail development — signals lender confidence in submarket at largest-ever single financing for Mag Mile Capital.

Downtown Fort Lauderdale's urban core projected to grow 26-28% by 2028-2030, outpacing Miami, West Palm Beach, and Tampa. Net in-migration from NY, DC, SF, LA/SD continues, providing structural renter demand foundation.

Florida's permanent state preemption of local rent control (since 1977) eliminates a key underwriting risk. Flagler Village's active Opportunity Zone status provides tax incentive for continued developer interest.

Professional and business services employment in the Fort Lauderdale metro grew 6%+ YoY as of Q2 2025. Broward County added 3,400 nonfarm jobs YoY through Dec 2025, outpacing the national 1.1% average with a 1.5% rate.
Fort Lauderdale cap rate at 6.27% with commercial mortgage rates at ~5.11% (Oct 2025) — positive leverage spread exists, though thin. Bridge capital actively deploying in the submarket.

Studio apartments bucking the trend with +11% YoY rent growth in Flagler Village, suggesting strong demand from young professionals. 1BR and 2BR units absorbing more concession pressure as larger new supply delivers.

feature_gaps_identified.gap: No access to granular Broward County lis pendens data for commercial multifamily properties. County recorder database not machine-accessible; could not quantify CRE-specific distress in Flagler Village. Would require direct PropertyShark or CoStar subscription or county scrape.

Fort Lauderdale permit portal (LauderBuild/Accela) does not expose structured permit data via public API. Could not generate actual permits-YTD count or track permit velocity for Flagler Village. A scraping integration or city data partnership is needed.

No submarket-level occupancy rate by vintage class (Class A vs B vs C) for Flagler Village. CoStar/Yardi Matrix would provide this; free sources only give city-wide approximations. Occupancy rate is a critical input for lease-up projection.

No 2025 closed land comp pricing with price-per-unit or price-per-sf on a consistent basis. All comp data found is from 2021-2023. A CoStar comparable sales integration would fill this gap and enable current land value underwriting.

No CMBS delinquency rate specific to Fort Lauderdale/Broward MSA for multifamily. TREPP or Bloomberg data needed. Could not assess whether any existing securitized loans in Flagler Village are in special servicing.

No supply absorption projection model — can estimate unit delivery count but cannot project how quickly 1,525+ new units will be absorbed given current leasing velocity. A Monte Carlo or cohort absorption model would materially improve acquisition timing recommendations.

No rent growth by property class (Class A vs B vs C) for Flagler Village. Studio trend (+11% YoY) vs 1BR (-7%) suggests vintage and unit mix matter significantly. Granular rent roll data by vintage would enable better rent escalation modeling.
